title: carex olivacea subsp. olivacea
intro: Carex olivacea subsp. olivacea, also known as the Olive Sedge, is a type of sedge plant that belongs to the Carex genus and is native to North America. The leaves and stems of this plant have been used in traditional medicine for their medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory and antiseptic properties that can help to soothe and heal wounds. The health benefits of Carex olivacea subsp. olivacea include reducing inflammation, promoting wound healing, and acting as an antimicrobial agent to prevent infection. The plant can be prepared as a tea or infusion by steeping the leaves and stems in hot water, or it can be used as a tincture or salve to apply topically to affected areas.
